etymology.description: 品(品) (pǐn, "three mouths") + 木(木) (mù, "tree") = chirping of birds in a tree -> sound of chirping
etymology.interpretation: The character 喿(zào) represents the sound of birds chirping from a tree. The combination of three mouths (品) implies multiple sounds, while the tree (木) indicates the setting of this sound, capturing the essence of birdsong emanating from the branches.
